concerns.text: Tasks T045-T050 introduce 'Planar Aromaticity Score', 'H-Bond Stabilization Energy', and 'Lattice Energy Estimates' derived from external heuristics or unverified COD metadata. This violates Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) and Principle VI (Open Crystallographic Data Integrity) by introducing synthetic/estimated data not present in raw CIFs or derivable from Bondi radii/SMILES alone, effectively weakening the 'Verified Accuracy' constraint.

Task T028 implements VIF diagnostics on 'PCA-reduced fingerprint components' and omits raw dimensions. This violates FR-009 which mandates VIF on 'all predictor variables'. Reducing dimensionality before VIF prevents detection of multicollinearity within the high-dimensional embedding space, failing the specific requirement.

Task T033 describes sweeping thresholds over 'a range of values' without specifying the set. FR-007 and SC-004 explicitly mandate the set {0.5, 0.6, 0.7}. The vague description risks implementing an incorrect set, violating the specific acceptance criteria.

Task T027 allows a fallback to '[deferred] shuffles' if runtime exceeds 3.5 hours. FR-016 and FR-006 mandate 10,000 shuffles for the final inference. While the plan mentions a conditional test, the task description implies a hard cap that could result in a non-compliant p-value resolution (worse than 0.0001) without explicit spec authorization for the reduction in the final report.

Tasks T037a-c introduce a 'Linear Regression Baseline' and specific ablation comparisons not listed in spec.md requirements or Success Criteria. While addressing reviewer feedback, these tasks implement unverified scope that dilutes the mandatory MLP architecture and specific metrics defined in FR-005/FR-006, risking orphan tasks that do not map to a specific FR.
